meta.titleDefault: Dr. Jens Richter (PhD), Physicist, Developer, Engineer
meta.description: Dr. Jens Richter, CEO of DNA Evolutions and a hands-on engineer. I build JOpt, a Java route-optimization engine, turning messy real-world logistics into feasible, optimal plans. PhD in silicon photonics, RWTH Aachen.

hero.tagline: A Physicist in Mind, Developer by Heart, Engineer by Passion.
hero.imageAlt: Illustrated split portrait of Dr. Jens Richter: a red, sketch-and-lightbulb left side for creative work; a blue, DNA-and-data right side for engineering; and, at the center, a warm scene of a family walking a path together.

doors.workAria: Go to Work: DNA Evolutions and the JOpt optimization engine

about.title: About
about.p1: Born in Dresden. Raised in Leverkusen. In Aachen I studied physics and computer science, then earned a doctorate (PhD) in electrical engineering.
about.now: I live in Eschweiler near Aachen, with my wife, my son, and our dog.
about.goal: Solve as many interesting problems as possible, subject to the constraint that something usable comes out at the end.

work.title: Now / Work
work.lead: I'm CEO of DNA Evolutions, and still work with source code.
work.body: We build JOpt, a route and tour optimization engine written in Java. It turns chaotic real-world logistics (vehicles, drivers, time windows, skills, hard constraints, multi-day planning) into feasible, optimal tours. Available natively in Java and as a Spring WebFlux REST service.
work.aside: Something has to decide who drives where and when, without breaking the rules. That something is a solver. And I build it.
work.companyAria: DNA Evolutions (opens in a new tab)
work.githubAria: DNA Evolutions on GitHub (opens in a new tab)

research.title: Research
research.lead: Doctorate: Dr.-Ing. in Electrical Engineering, RWTH Aachen, 2018.
research.plain: In plain terms: I built tiny glass rings on a silicon chip that trap light and let it loop millions of times. That's the basis for very precise optical measurements and for optical frequency combs, essentially rulers made of light. Programming and quantum physics were my daily companions.
research.thesisTitle: Ultra-high-Q inverted silica microtoroid resonators monolithically integrated into a silicon photonics platform.
research.recordAria: Official record: RWTH Aachen thesis (opens in a new tab)
